body:
[powerlevel10k](https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k) used [gitstatus](https://github.com/romkatv/gitstatus) to speed up its git related stuffs.

I don't know if starship can use gitstatus also in background for git?

EDIT: fun fact, even use `starship` on its own project *this*, it's already a bit noticeable lag :(

Pretty much lag on this project with 2k8 commit:
```
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")

$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
$(/etc/profiles/per-user/user/bin/starship prompt --right --terminal-width="$COLUMNS" --keymap="${KEYMAP:-}" --status="$STARSHIP_CMD_STATUS" --pipestatus="${STARSHIP_PIPE_STATUS[*]}" --cmd-duration="${STARSHIP_DURATION:-}" --jobs="$STARSHIP_JOBS_COUNT")
```

```
 git_status  -  125ms  -   ""
```

Thanks for the report, the performance of `git_status` is already being discussed in #4305, so I will close this issue to keep discussion in one place. Enabling certain git features (`core.fsmonitor`, `feature.manyFiles`, or using `scalar`) can also help avoid performance issues.

You can also improve the performance by configuring the `git_status` module to ignore submodules and not to show untracked files:
```toml
[git_status]
untracked = ''
ignore_submodules = true
```

> git_status  -  125ms  -   ""

Down from `125ms` to `85ms` on this project, but still lag,

If we can't come up with any idea or can just allow user to disable completely git_* related stuffs on a repo that break the timeout for good.

otherwise, this is unusable.

> Thanks for the report, the performance of `git_status` is already being discussed in #4305, so I will close this issue to keep discussion in one place. Enabling certain git features (`core.fsmonitor`, `feature.manyFiles`, or using `scalar`) can also help avoid performance issues.
> 
> You can also improve the performance by configuring the `git_status` module to ignore submodules and not to show untracked files:
> 
> ```toml
> [git_status]
> untracked = ''
> ignore_submodules = true
> ```
